VAULTS.Sync.NothingToDo: Vault is already up to date.
VAULTS.Sync.TokenExpired: Vault token for {name} has expired. Click Authenticate, then Sync again.
VAULTS.Connect.StepOpen: Open the vault sign-in page in your browser:
VAULTS.Connect.StepPaste: Paste the token shown back here:
VAULTS.Connect.PasteInvalid: That doesn't look like a token. Tokens have three dot-separated parts.
VAULTS.Connect.PasteExpired: That token has already expired. Open the sign-in page again to get a fresh one.
VAULTS.Dialog.Title: Vaults
VAULTS.Dialog.RemoveHint: Remove deletes this vault's journals and image cache from the world. The vault on the server is not affected.
VAULTS.Dialog.RemoveConfirmBody: Delete all journals and cached images for {name}? This can't be undone (you can re-sync to restore).
VAULTS.Dialog.DmRoleHint: Pages whose role is below this tier are imported as player-visible (Observer ownership). Pages at this tier or higher stay GM-only.
VAULTS.Dialog.HandlerAssetsHint: Optional: pull handler-shipped CSS/JS from this vault into Foundry. Each handler also has to opt in (assets.foundry.{styles,scripts}). Off by default.
VAULTS.HandlerAssets.WarnIntro: You're about to enable handler-asset import for "{name}". The vault's deployed wiki may ship these assets:
VAULTS.HandlerAssets.WarnStyles: CSS will be injected into Foundry. At worst this restyles a journal sheet.
VAULTS.HandlerAssets.WarnScripts: JavaScript will run inside Foundry. It can read and modify game state, hook into combat, alter actor data, or call out to external URLs. Treat this like installing a Foundry module from the same author.
VAULTS.HandlerAssets.WarnTrust: Only enable this if you trust whoever authored the handlers in this vault.
VAULTS.HandlerAssets.WarnReversible: You can turn this off at any time and reload the world to undo the injection.
VAULTS.HandlerAssets.SessionPromptTitle: Run handler scripts from this vault?
VAULTS.HandlerAssets.SessionPromptIntro: Vault "{name}" is about to run {size} KB of JavaScript inside this Foundry session ({reason}).
VAULTS.HandlerAssets.SessionPromptWarn: This script will run with full access to game state, hooks, and document data. It can read player input, modify actors, or call external services.
VAULTS.HandlerAssets.SessionPromptDeclin: If you decline, the persistent setting stays on; you'll be asked again next time the vault syncs (or on next world load).
